disclaimer: Community Meme Score reflects recurring player-community jokes about card packages. It is not an objective measure of skill.

categories.panic_button: Cards commonly joked about as emergency defence buttons.
categories.midladder_landlord: Cards associated with mid-ladder defensive comfort picks.
categories.defensive_spam: Lane-filling or structure-heavy cards some players find annoying.
categories.overlevel_bully: Cards that become louder when overlevelled in ladder contexts.
categories.bridge_spam: Fast pressure cards that repeatedly ask the tower the same question.
categories.annoyance_engine: Cards that generate repeated nuisance value or force awkward answers.
categories.bait_menace: Packages that turn small-spell timing into customer support.
categories.copy_paste_meta: Recognised packages with strong community identity.
categories.high_elixir_prayer: Heavy commitments where the plan can become hope with a receipt.
categories.group_project_deck: Several unrelated meme traits stacked without a clear archetype.

cards.name: Mega Knight
roast_directions: U watch Pay Gorn on a daily basis
Dropped from the ceiling whenever a normal defence sounded difficult
Mega Knight customer-support department
cards.source_notes: Mega Knight is commonly discussed as an overlevelled or mid-ladder panic-button meme, while counterplay and balance context remain subjective.
cards.disclaimer_text: This is a community joke tag, not proof of objective card strength or player skill.

cards.name: Royal Recruits
roast_directions: I've seen Pay Gorn straighter than this
Defence by zoning ordinance
Royal Recruits were deployed because apparently one lane was not annoying enough
cards.source_notes: Royal Recruits are often perceived by some players as defensive lane spam or unfun lane-filling pressure, but that is a subjective complaint.
cards.disclaimer_text: The tag records a player-community stereotype, not an official card judgement.

cards.name: Elite Barbarians
roast_directions: Two angry gym bros sprinting at the bridge
The plan was apparently just louder Barbarians
cards.source_notes: Elite Barbarians are a recurring ladder meme when overlevelled bridge pressure is the whole conversation.
cards.disclaimer_text: This is playful community shorthand, not a balance claim.

cards.name: Wizard
roast_directions: Five-elixir fireball intern
You hired Wizard because subtlety was not in the budget
cards.source_notes: Wizard is often framed as a mid-ladder support-card meme, especially when copied into expensive splash piles.
cards.disclaimer_text: This describes a meme context, not a universal weakness.

cards.name: Witch
roast_directions: Skeleton daycare supervisor
Summoning paperwork until the opponent gets bored
cards.source_notes: Witch is commonly joked about as skeleton paperwork in mid-ladder decks, with context-dependent usefulness.

cards.name: Firecracker
roast_directions: Backwards-walking HR complaint
One rocket, three tower activations, somehow still invited back
cards.source_notes: Firecracker is a frequent annoyance meme because of long-range splash and movement quirks.
cards.disclaimer_text: This is not an objective measure of skill.

cards.name: Hog Rider
roast_directions: Repeatedly yelling at a building
The same man on a pig has filed another tower complaint
cards.source_notes: Hog Rider is a classic tower-pressure meme; recognised cycle decks should be treated as real archetypes, not automatic fraud.
cards.disclaimer_text: Context matters; this tag is playful.

cards.name: Electro Giant
roast_directions: Walking electrical outage
You spent eight elixir on a mobile power cut and hoped diplomacy would follow
cards.source_notes: Electro Giant is often joked about as a huge all-in walking outage.

cards.name: P.E.K.K.A
roast_directions: One massive adult solving a card game by standing on it
The elixir bar has been declared a non-renewable resource
cards.source_notes: P.E.K.K.A can be a legitimate tank answer; the meme tag applies when stacked with other huge panic cards.
cards.disclaimer_text: Do not treat the tag as a standalone skill claim.

cards.name: Royal Giant
roast_directions: Tower paperwork from six tiles away
A building-targeting complaint in crown form
cards.source_notes: Royal Giant has a long-running community identity as tower pressure, but recognised control shells can be real meta decks.

cards.name: X-Bow
roast_directions: A building applied for tower access and you approved the permit
You turned Clash Royale into municipal planning
cards.source_notes: X-Bow is a recognised archetype and a long-running frustration meme; score it with context.
cards.disclaimer_text: This is a meme receipt, not a balance finding.

cards.name: Mortar
roast_directions: You brought artillery to a card game
The tower is being evicted from another postcode
cards.source_notes: Mortar is both a real archetype and an annoyance meme depending on package.

cards.name: Lava Hound
roast_directions: Floating dog aviation department
Airspace violation paperwork
cards.source_notes: Lava Hound becomes a meme mostly as part of air-mail packages like LavaLoon.

cards.name: Balloon
roast_directions: Bomb delivery with no customer-service number
Aviation, but make it tower damage
cards.source_notes: Balloon is a pressure card with meme context when paired into repeated air-mail all-ins.

roast_directions: Mega Knight plus Royal Recruits. Bro did not build a deck; bro built a Gay Strip Club.
You dropped a landlord from the ceiling, then hired eight shielded tenants to block both lanes.
This is the kind of deck that sees a normal interaction and responds by filing a zoning complaint.
Mega Knight and Royal Recruits together: two panic buttons, one elixir bar, zero desire to have a normal match.
Certified mid-ladder landlord behaviour.
combinations.source_notes: A deliberately stronger package because both cards in the same current or recent-main deck create a clear defensive-spam/panic-button meme pattern.
combinations.disclaimer_text: Still a joke receipt, not an official skill verdict.

roast_directions: Your deck has become a customer-service test for people holding The Log.
You are sending goblins through the post and calling it strategy.
combinations.source_notes: Classic bait packages are real archetypes, but also recurring community annoyance memes.
combinations.disclaimer_text: Recognised archetypes should not be scored as max fraud by default.

roast_directions: Two massive adults solving a card game by standing on it.
The elixir bar has been declared a non-renewable resource.
combinations.source_notes: Heavy-card stacking can be a community meme when two large emergency cards share one deck.

roast_directions: The fisherman dragged the game plan into place and Royal Giant did the paperwork.
combinations.source_notes: Royal Giant plus Fisherman is a recognised shell with both meta legitimacy and community-meme identity.
combinations.disclaimer_text: Do not over-score exact recognised meta shells.

roast_directions: Airspace violation package.
You sent a floating dog and a bomb toward the tower and called it aviation.
combinations.source_notes: LavaLoon is a real archetype and a familiar all-in air-pressure joke.
